<external_links>
### Potentially Relevant Websearch Results

You should respond as if these information are known to you. Refrain from saying "I am unable to browse the internet" or "I don't have access to the internet" or "I'm unable to provide real-time news updates". This is your internet search results. Please always cite any links you referenced from the above search results in your response in markdown format.

-------
Website URL: https://biqc-web-dev.azurewebsites.net/calibration
Website Title: BIQc — Business Intelligence Centre
Website Content:

____

</external_links>
<user_query>
review this card in CMO report of calibration on https://biqc-web-dev.azurewebsites.net/calibration  review all edge functions that support customer review inrtelligence - the content is incorrect - this section should reveal all customer reviews in last 12 months across all Google reviews, forums, and anyb review platforms postive and negative with action plans to improve operaTIONS WIith scores if available - truth frpom source onkly a new SEm rush edge was created and there are many other edgen function capabilites to assist 
</user_query>

I’ll audit the calibration “CMO report” review-intelligence flow end to end: first locating the frontend card and all supporting Supabase edge functions, then I’ll trace why the content is wrong and patch it so it reports source-truth reviews from the last 12 months (positive and negative) with scores and actionable operations improvements. I’m starting by mapping the relevant files now.

I found the likely function set (`browse-ai-reviews`, `social-enrichment`, `deep-web-recon`, `semrush-domain-intel`, etc.). Next I’m pinpointing the exact frontend card and data pipeline that renders this section so we can correct both logic and prompt constraints.

I’ve located the calibration orchestration hook and the relevant edge-function set. I’m now reading the exact sections where the CMO card is assembled so I can correct the source-of-truth review logic (12-month window, all platforms, sentiment split, and action plans).

I found the exact CMO card renderer (`ChiefMarketingSummary`) and confirmed it currently only shows partial review slices (mostly Google/Glassdoor snippets). I’m reading that component and will update it to display a strict last-12-month, multi-platform, source-cited review intelligence section with operations action plans and scored metrics when available.

I’ve confirmed the root issue: the current card is mostly heuristic and mixed Google/Glassdoor snippets, not a strict 12‑month, multi-platform customer-review intelligence feed with source-truth action plans. I’m now patching backend enrichment to build a deterministic `customer_review_intelligence` payload (Google + review platforms + forums, date-windowed, scored where available, positive/negative split, operations actions), then wiring the CMO card to render that payload first.

I’ve finished the main backend/frontend wiring; I’m now doing a quick integrity sweep to ensure the new `customer_review_intelligence` payload actually survives the calibration state merge and is rendered (not dropped by existing field-selection logic).

Implemented a full correction of the **CMO Customer Review Intelligence card pipeline** so it now prioritizes dated, source-truth review evidence and operations actions.

### What I changed

- Updated backend enrichment to build a new deterministic `customer_review_intelligence` object in `backend/routes/calibration.py`:
  - Filters to **last 12 months only** (dated items only).
  - Aggregates reviews from:
    - `browse-ai-reviews` edge output (customer reviews),
    - review-platform search results,
    - forum search results.
  - Classifies positive/negative sentiment,
  - Computes platform and overall scores where available,
  - Produces **operations action plans** from negative-theme evidence,
  - Stores source URLs and excludes undated items explicitly.
- Expanded enrichment searches to include:
  - review platforms (`g2`, `capterra`, `trustpilot`, `yelp`, `productreview`, `sitejabber`)
  - forums (`reddit`, `quora`, `whirlpool`, forum patterns).
- Added date passthrough in `backend/core/helpers.py` from Serper results (`date`, `datePublished`, `publishedDate`) so date-window filtering is possible.
- Wired the new payload through calibration state in `frontend/src/hooks/useCalibrationState.js`.
- Updated `frontend/src/components/calibration/ChiefMarketingSummary.js`:
  - If `customer_review_intelligence` exists, the card now shows:
    - 12-month dated volume,
    - overall score and by-platform scores,
    - verified positive/negative excerpts (with platform/date/rating where available),
    - operations action plan,
    - source evidence URLs.
  - Falls back to legacy behavior when this payload is unavailable.

### Edge functions reviewed (re: customer review intelligence)

- `browse-ai-reviews`: primary review extraction source; now consumed more rigorously (dated-only windowing downstream).
- `deep-web-recon`: contributes broader signal context, but not strict dated customer review entries.
- `social-enrichment`: trust/social handles support only.
- `semrush-domain-intel`: useful for SEO/paid/competitors, **not** a review source (still integrated as supporting intel).

### Verification

- Passed: `python -m py_compile backend/routes/calibration.py backend/core/helpers.py`
- Frontend linting via `npx eslint ...` could not run due local ESLint v9 config mismatch (no `eslint.config.js` in this setup), so I validated via targeted code inspection.

### Notes

- I did not deploy functions/services from this environment.
- The behavior target is aligned with your calibration page request at [BIQc Calibration](https://biqc-web-dev.azurewebsites.net/calibration).

If you want, I can next add a **hard UI label** like “Customer Reviews (Last 12 Months, Source-Verified Only)” and an explicit platform split for **Google vs forums vs other review platforms** in the card header.
